title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York (hybrid, globally distributed)
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine. Requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling RevOps beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, plus collaboration across time zones with the Tel Aviv HQ. A hands-on leader-doer who builds flows, dashboards, and data models.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment: 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, senior RevOps background matches Head of role perfectly, B2B SaaS and Fintech expertise directly applicable to cloud cost management, Salesforce admin and pipeline forecasting skills core to the GTM engine build. Location is ideal with Tel Aviv HQ matching preferred area and global remote distribution.
ai_opener: I've been scaling RevOps functions across high-growth B2B SaaS and Fintech for 15+ years, and Finout's mission to operationalize cloud cost visibility resonates deeply—especially given how RevOps teams themselves struggle with SaaS spend optimization. Your GTM engine rebuild at this inflection point aligns perfectly with my hands-on approach to building forecasting models, Salesforce architectures, and cross-timezone operational cadences. I'd love to explore how we can accelerate your path to $100M+ ARR.

title: Revenue Operations Manager - Systems
description: Flex seeks a Revenue Operations Manager to build, own, and scale the operational backbone for its New Verticals division. Reporting to the Head of Revenue Operations, this first dedicated RevOps hire will stand up the systems, processes, and data infrastructure to power the division. Requires 5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM Operations with 2+ years in a senior/lead capacity, plus deep Salesforce expertise configuring and building environments. Familiarity with Outreach, LeanData, Clay, and HubSpot is valued. A builder mentality energized by creating from zero.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps expertise, deep Salesforce capabilities, and proven track record building GTM infrastructure from zero. Remote role in target geography, though this is an individual contributor position rather than his typical leadership scope.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue operations at scale in B2B SaaS, and your New Verticals RevOps build resonates deeply—I've built similar zero-to-one Salesforce ecosystems with Outreach, LeanData, and HubSpot integrations. The opportunity to be the first dedicated RevOps hire designing operational infrastructure for a new division aligns perfectly with how I think about scalable GTM systems. Would love to discuss how I can accelerate Flex's vertical expansion through data-driven operations.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, the AI-powered revenue intelligence platform serving 5,000+ companies, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations. You'll architect the territory strategy framework, manage operating cadence, and partner with Sales leadership, Strategy, and Finance to optimize territory composition. Responsibilities include analyzing historical performance, market potential, and account data to inform segmentation; delivering whitespace analysis and coverage recommendations; and building dashboards and reporting tools. The annual salary range is $149,000-$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent match: RevOps Manager role at scale (5000+ customers) with core GTM responsibilities (territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, sales ops). Remote US role aligns with location preferences, and Gong's AI-native platform perfectly fits Omri's strong RevOps + GTM background.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting revenue operations and GTM strategies at scale in B2B SaaS, and I've been impressed by how Gong has positioned itself as the nerve center for revenue intelligence. Your Territory Strategy & Operations opening caught my attention because it's exactly the intersection of data-driven segmentation, forecasting rigor, and sales leadership alignment I've built my career around. I'd love to explore how my experience optimizing territory composition and building predictive dashboards could accelerate Gong's operating model.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Platomics, a healthcare-focused tech company, seeks a RevOps Manager to administer its CRM (HubSpot) and RevOps tools, ensuring integrations, data hygiene, and system reliability. You'll partner with sales, marketing, and finance on goals, metrics, and revenue strategies, support forecasting, territory planning, and quota setting, and train teams on processes. Requires 3+ years in revenue operations, sales operations, or business analytics in tech (SaaS, cloud, AI). This is a part-time position (20h/week) with possibility to move to full-time in 2027. Salary €25,000-€31,000.
score_reason: Strong technical fit (HubSpot, RevOps fundamentals, B2B SaaS) and role aligns with Omri's expertise, but significant concerns: part-time structure (20h/week) mismatches a senior executive profile, modest salary range suggests junior positioning, and healthcare vertical is outside proven fintech/AI-native GTM experience.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years scaling revenue operations in B2B SaaS and fintech, and I'm impressed by Platomics' focus on healthcare tech. Your RevOps Manager role hits my wheelhouse on HubSpot, forecasting, and cross-functional alignment, though I want to understand if you're open to restructuring this as a full-time embedded partnership rather than part-time—I find that depth matters most when building scalable GTM infrastructure.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: VulnCheck seeks a Revenue Operations Manager to design and optimize scalable revenue processes across the full customer lifecycle. Requires 4+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or GTM Systems in B2B SaaS, hands-on Salesforce administration (or HubSpot lifecycle/lead scoring), and experience building executive dashboards covering pipeline health, conversion, forecasting, bookings, and churn. Strong knowledge of SaaS metrics like ARR, ACV, sales velocity, pipeline coverage, NRR, and churn required, plus experience integrating GTM tech stack tools including enrichment, sales engagement, and BI platforms.
score_reason: Exceptional match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds 4+ year requirement, deep Salesforce/HubSpot expertise, proven GTM systems and SaaS metrics mastery (ARR/ACV/NRR/churn), and demonstrated ability to build executive dashboards and integrate complex tech stacks. Remote US location is ideal for this candidate.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years building scalable RevOps engines for B2B SaaS companies, and VulnCheck's focus on designing repeatable revenue processes across the full lifecycle resonates deeply with my experience. I've architected Salesforce/HubSpot implementations, built executive dashboards for pipeline forecasting and churn analysis, and integrated complex GTM tech stacks—exactly what you're looking for in a Revenue Operations Manager.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team effective. Conveo is scaling rapidly, and this role replaces patchwork SaaS tools and manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows. Responsibilities include building lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows that activate customers faster, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly.
score_reason: Strong GTM alignment and remote location fit, but the role's emphasis on coding and building internal tools diverges from Omri's core RevOps/operations management expertise. Better suited for a technical RevOps engineer than a senior GTM executive.
ai_opener: Conveo's approach to replacing manual GTM workflows with AI-powered automation resonates with modern revenue operations—but I'd want to understand whether you're seeking someone to architect the RevOps strategy and tool stack, or primarily to code and ship internal tools. My strength is designing scalable GTM systems and leading teams through rapid scaling phases; if the role is more engineering-focused, a full-stack technical operator might be the better fit.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pylon seeks a GTM Engineer to test, implement, and optimize an AI outbound solution from setup through performance tuning. You'll build automated outbound sequences ensuring deliverability and personalization at scale, own email infrastructure including domain health and warming strategies, create data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling, collaborate with RevOps on lead scoring and routing, and ship growth experiments like website widgets and ROI calculators. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM engineering/growth roles at B2B SaaS companies, plus strong technical skills with APIs and webhooks.
score_reason: Exceptional match: GTM Engineer role directly aligns with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, and the AI-native outbound solution focus matches his strong interest in AI-native GTM. The technical stack (APIs, webhooks, marketing automation, CRM integration, lead scoring) is core to his skillset, though the 3+ year requirement is well below his 15+ year seniority.
ai_opener: Omri here—I noticed Pylon's building AI-powered outbound infrastructure that bridges marketing automation, CRM, and sales operations. With 15+ years optimizing B2B SaaS GTM stacks and deep experience in email deliverability, lead scoring, and data pipeline architecture, I'm interested in how you're approaching personalization at scale without sacrificing sender reputation. Worth a conversation about GTM engineer opportunities?

title: GTM Engineer
description: FacilityOS, a B2B SaaS company providing a facility management platform for mid-to-large enterprises, is weaving AI into its GTM motion across support, success, sales, and marketing. Your GTM Engineer role looks like it's solving exactly that: designing systems that connect data, automation, and human decision-making.
